methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced PMU's IT policy statement, official AI center member page, Artificial Intelligence program pages, and the PMU homepage; it did not surface a central university-wide generative AI policy page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:pmu.edu.sa "ChatGPT" "Prince Mohammad Bin Fahd" OR PMU policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search results were PMU faculty profile pages and a law blog item about ChatGPT privacy, not a formal university AI-use policy.
Firecrawl search results were faculty profiles, a faculty-uploaded paper, a conference page, and a futures studies conference item; these were not student/faculty policy sources.
Firecrawl search surfaced PMU's CCES Journal publication policies with an AI Usage Policy, the PMU academic catalog PDF, symposium and brochure PDFs, and AI program/course pages.
Firecrawl search identified the official PMU Academic Catalog PDF and related policy/procedure PDFs for academic integrity context.
robots.txt was fetched by plain HTTP. It disallows administrative and selected attachment paths, but does not disallow the pages and PDF used in this run.

artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery found no accessible central PMU-wide generative AI or ChatGPT policy for students or faculty. Usable official evidence came from PMU's IT policy statement, PMU's academic catalog academic-integrity section, and a narrow CCES Journal AI Usage Policy. AI center, AI academic program, event, blog, and homepage sources were recorded as rejected or limited because they do not provide central institutional AI-use rules.

artifacts.claimText: PMU's CCES Journal publication policies permit authors to use AI tools for research assistance, data analysis, language editing, and formatting, while requiring transparent disclosure and keeping authors responsible for the work.

citation.citationTitle: Publication Policies CCES Journal :: PMU, Prince Mohammad Bin Fahd University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Publication Policies CCES Journal :: PMU, Prince Mohammad Bin Fahd University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Authors may use AI tools for: Research assistance; Data analysis; Language editing and formatting. However: The use of AI tools must be transparently disclosed in the manuscript. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, originality, integrity, and ethical compliance of the work.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The CCES Journal AI policy allows AI tools for research assistance, data analysis, language editing, and formatting, but requires disclosure and author responsibility.

artifacts.title: Prince Mohammad Bin Fahd University AI Policy Crawl Draft
artifacts.summary: Official PMU discovery did not locate a central public university-wide generative AI policy for students or faculty. The crawl found a narrow AI Usage Policy in PMU's CCES Journal publication policies, general student academic-integrity provisions in the PMU Academic Catalog, and general IT acceptable-use/privacy language in the PMU IT policy statement. AI center, AI program, homepage, blog, and event sources were rejected or treated as limited because they were not policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The CCES Journal AI Usage Policy is journal-submission scoped and should not be generalized as a central PMU student or faculty generative AI policy.
No login-gated, third-party, event-only, or research-showcase source was used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
